question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/httpErrorHandler.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: handleError()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes `req.ip` to be an extremely long string (e.g., via a spoofed X-Forwarded-For header with thousands of IPs), causing the log string in `handleError()` to be unboundedly large and exhaust logger memory or disk?
